title: NQBlade Backtest Results (July 2025 – February 2026)
author: Some_Fly_4552
selftext:
Hey everyone,

I wanted to share a backtest of NQBlade, my NAS100 trading bot.

This test covers the period from July 2025 to February 2026 and gives a good overview of how the bot performs across different market phases.

About the strategy:

NQBlade trades NAS100 on the 3-minute chart using a structured trend-following approach. It combines moving averages, volatility filters, and higher-timeframe confirmation to align entries with the broader market direction instead of taking random short-term signals.

Each setup is split into 4 partial positions, which allows the bot to secure profits step by step while still leaving room for larger runners. On top of that, session filters and trading-day filters help avoid lower-quality market conditions.

What you’re seeing in this backtest:

	•	Backtest period: July 2025 – February 2026

	•	Market: NAS100

	•	Execution on the 3-minute timeframe

	•	Multi-position trade management

	•	Trend-following logic with filtered entries

This is not meant to show a “perfect system,” but rather to give a transparent look at how the bot behaves over a longer sample size.

If you have questions about the strategy, trade management, or setup logic, feel free to comment or DM me

comments.author: Scary_T13
comments.body: Looks good, thanks for sharing. What system do you use for backtesting your strategy?

comments.author: Patient_Kangaroo4864
comments.body:
Thanks for sharing this — always appreciate when people post actual test windows instead of just equity curve screenshots.

A few questions that would help evaluate it better:

- What were the key metrics over that period? (Net return, max drawdown, profit factor, win rate, average R multiple, etc.)
- Were commissions and realistic slippage included for NQ/CFD fills on a 3-min chart?
- How did it perform during high-impact news or strong trend reversal days?
- Is the higher-timeframe confirmation fixed (e.g., 1H/4H), or adaptive?

Also, July 2025–Feb 2026 is a decent sample, but still relatively short and likely regime-specific. Have you tested it across:
- A low-volatility grind period
- A high-volatility expansion phase
- A choppy range-bound environment?

Trend-following on NQ can look amazing in clean directional months and then give a lot back in compression. Curious how your volatility filter handles that.

If you're open to sharing, an equity curve + drawdown curve and a trade distribution breakdown would make this much easier to assess objectively.

Solid start though — structured trend alignment on a lower timeframe makes sense if execution and costs are tightly controlled.
